title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background is an exceptionally strong domain match — hybrid AI search, search ranking/relevance, personalization, recommendation systems, and A/B experimentation at Samsung.com and Amazon are precisely what Airbnb's R&P team needs. The 9 years of experience falls just shy of the 10+ year requirement (a minor delta), but the depth of ML-adjacent PM work on systems serving 10M+ users is compelling evidence. The slight seniority gap (role signals Staff/Lead level with strategic ownership of a flagship AI platform) is the one honest stretch, but Riley's defined product vision and roadmap at Samsung puts this firmly in "seriously apply" territory.
recommendation_reason: Near-perfect domain alignment across search relevance, personalization, recommendations, and conversational AI — Riley's resume reads like a prerequisite checklist for this role.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping search ranking, personalization, and recommendation systems at global scale — including hybrid AI search across 3 international markets at Samsung.com and ML-driven catalog systems at Amazon. Proven track record defining product vision, owning end-to-end roadmaps, and partnering with ML engineers and applied researchers to drive measurable marketplace outcomes including +45% 1st-result CTR and +15% AOV on a $2B+ GMV platform.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%.
gap_handling: The 10+ year requirement is a soft gap (Riley has 9 years) — lead with depth of ML-adjacent ownership rather than total years. The role also signals Staff/Lead-level strategic ownership of a flagship AI platform; Riley should explicitly frame Samsung work as setting multi-year product vision and making hard prioritization tradeoffs across competing objectives (not just shipping features), which the resume partially does but can be sharpened. Airbnb's context is two-sided marketplace health (guest + host) — adding even one sentence about balancing multi-stakeholder tradeoffs in a marketplace context will close the framing gap.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's core competencies — AI-powered discovery, search ranking, personalization, recommendation systems, and A/B experimentation at scale — map almost perfectly to what Airbnb is building on its homepage. The Samsung experience (10M+ monthly users, hybrid search, conversational AI, product discovery funnels) and the Kolon e-commerce background (recommendation systems, CTR optimization, conversion funnel) provide direct, quantified evidence for this exact domain. The primary gap is consumer marketplace / travel context (Airbnb's unique supply-demand two-sided marketplace) and a modest stretch on the "product narrative / marketing partnership" axis, which Riley has touched but not headlined.
recommendation_reason: 9 years of AI-powered discovery and search at consumer scale is a near-perfect domain match; strong quantified evidence across every core requirement.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping search, discovery, and personalization experiences at consumer scale — including a hybrid AI search platform serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven recommendation systems on a $2B+ GMV e-commerce platform. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers, data scientists, and UX teams to build AI-powered browsing experiences that measurably lift engagement, conversion, and retention.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 → $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
gap_handling: The JD emphasizes "product narrative / marketing storytelling" and lifecycle campaign partnership — areas Riley has touched (AEO pipeline, conversational AI GTM, CRM marketing) but not headlined. In the resume and cover letter, explicitly frame the AEO pipeline launch (8x AI agent traffic, +40% conversion in 12 weeks) and the conversational AI chat experience as examples of crafting product narrative and driving cross-functional marketing alignment, not just engineering delivery.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience maps strongly onto the 6+ year requirement, with direct proof points in e-commerce discovery, personalization/recommendation systems, A/B experimentation, and cross-functional delivery at scale (Samsung.com, Kolonmall). The consumer product domain — shopping experience, discovery, conversion optimization — is a near-perfect archetype match. The only meaningful gap is the health/nutrition/wellness focus of this specific product area, where Riley has no explicit experience, but that's a preferred qualification, not a hard requirement, and the broader e-commerce discovery toolkit transfers well.
recommendation_reason: Strong consumer e-commerce, discovery, and personalization background directly aligns with the Shopping Experience role; health domain is preferred, not required.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years building consumer e-commerce discovery and personalization experiences at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon, shipping AI-powered search, recommendation systems, and guided shopping flows for 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record taking zero-to-one bets from ambiguous discovery to scaled launch, with rigorous A/B experimentation and measurable impact on engagement, conversion, and retention.
top_bullets: Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy — increased PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reduced bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows.
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing.

Launched ML-powered virtual try-on feature with size recommendation, achieving 70% adoption rate with 40% reduction in return rate MoM by leading end-to-end product lifecycle from discovery through GTM execution.
gap_handling: The Health domain focus (dietary needs, nutrition, wellness) is Riley's only visible gap. Address this proactively in the cover letter by framing personalization and discovery work as inherently about surfacing the *right* product to the *right* user — a principle that maps directly to health-conscious discovery. If Riley has any personal interest in health/nutrition apps or wellness, even as a user, that's worth briefly mentioning to signal genuine curiosity about the problem space.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong technical match for this role — deep hands-on experience with hybrid search, ranking, relevance metrics, query understanding, and ML-powered discovery systems at Samsung and Amazon maps directly to Faire's search algorithm ownership. The wholesale/B2B marketplace domain is adjacent but not identical to Riley's consumer e-commerce background, and the role explicitly requires 7+ years at a "high-growth tech startup or as a startup founder," which is a modest gap given Riley's enterprise experience (Samsung, Kolon). The seniority title is Staff PM, which is a stretch above Riley's target Senior/PM III level, and the NYC/SF hybrid-in-office requirement adds friction for a Bellevue-based candidate.
recommendation_reason: Excellent search algorithm depth and ML credibility, but Staff-level seniority and hybrid NYC/SF office requirement are real friction points; apply if willing to relocate or negotiate remote.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered search and discovery systems at scale — including hybrid search ranking, query understanding, and ML-driven personalization serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record partnering with world-class data science and engineering teams to define relevance metrics, run rigorous experiments, and deliver measurable business outcomes across global e-commerce platforms.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The "high-growth tech startup" requirement is the trickiest gap — Samsung and Kolon are enterprise/large-corp environments. Counter this by leaning into the Amazon experience (high-growth, high-velocity culture) and emphasizing startup-like ownership: end-to-end delivery, founder-level decision-making, and fast iteration cycles. Avoid positioning yourself as an enterprise PM; frame your work as scrappy, metrics-driven, and high-impact.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, A/B testing discipline, and experience shipping ML-powered features at Samsung and Amazon are strong signals of competence for this role. However, the role is explicitly focused on developer-platform / API growth — a B2B/PLG funnel archetype — and Riley's portfolio is weighted toward consumer e-commerce discovery and search, with only one B2B platform role (Amazon seller tools) that wasn't growth-focused. The gap in dedicated developer-platform or API growth product experience (onboarding funnels, console UX, usage-based billing, self-serve upgrade paths) is real and is likely to be the primary screen filter.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and growth fundamentals, but a meaningful gap in developer-platform / API-product experience means this is a competitive stretch requiring deliberate resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving growth through ML-powered search, discovery, and personalization products at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon — shipping at scale for 10M+ users and 2M+ B2B sellers. Proven track record leading full-funnel experimentation, activation optimization, and self-serve workflows that directly map to API platform growth levers including onboarding, conversion, and monetization.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — led self-serve workflow enhancements that saved $1.4M in operational cost and improved Ease-of-Use score by 380bps.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% through model optimization and iterative A/B testing.
gap_handling: The core gap is developer-platform / API growth experience. To address this: (1) In your cover letter, explicitly bridge your Amazon B2B seller-tools work to developer-platform thinking — self-serve workflows, activation funnels, and structured data pipelines are directly analogous. (2) Reframe your AEO pipeline and recommendation API bullets to foreground the API/integration surface, not just the outcome. (3) If you have any side projects, personal API usage, or opinions on Claude's developer onboarding, weave that into your narrative to demonstrate genuine platform-side empathy.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience — spanning hybrid search, conversational AI, LLM pipelines, and RAG-adjacent work (AEO pipelines, recommendation APIs) — maps closely to the role's core focus on Copilot backend services and AI orchestration. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO metadata pipeline directly speak to the RAG and agent orchestration requirements. However, the role is explicitly titled "Principal PM" (a level Riley marks as "Avoid") and leans heavily into developer-platform and backend infrastructure domains (API platforms, SID error logs, B2B developer tooling) that differ from Riley's consumer/e-commerce discovery background. The 8+ years requirement is met, but the B2B developer/platform PM archetype is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM signal matches well, but the Principal title exceeds Riley's target seniority and the developer-platform/B2B backend focus is a meaningful archetype stretch from consumer e-commerce discovery.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI chat experiences and hybrid search pipelines to AEO metadata automation — that drive measurable engagement at scale. Proven track record orchestrating cross-functional AI product delivery across backend data pipelines, contextual personalization, and intelligent user assistance for audiences of 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The Principal title and B2B developer-platform focus are the two honest gaps here. For the title, Riley can lean into scope (10M+ users, multi-market launches, cross-org influence) to justify Principal-level impact without claiming the title directly. For the developer/B2B gap, the Amazon experience (2M+ B2B sellers, ML-driven listing pipelines, multi-modal classification) is the strongest bridge — lead with it when positioning for this role, and frame Samsung's internal tooling and AEO pipeline work as developer-adjacent backend product ownership.

The stretch is seniority: this is a Staff PM role, and while Riley's target includes Staff as a stretch, Airbnb's CS Platform framing (platform product, multi-surface infrastructure, cross-org alignment at Staff level) skews toward a more seasoned platform-builder — Riley's experience is more product-facing than infrastructure-platform-facing. No customer support / CS domain experience is also a gap the JD implicitly prizes.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI personalization and search signals, but this is a Staff-level platform infrastructure role in the CS domain — one level above Riley's target and outside her core domain; worth applying with tailored positioning.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered personalization and search systems at consumer scale — including hybrid search, LLM-driven pipelines, and recommendation platforms serving 10M+ monthly users. top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML foundation and measurable impact, but Staff-level platform PM with agentic systems depth is a genuine stretch from Riley's consumer-product-facing background.

top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, A/B testing rigor, and scale credentials (10M+ users, $2B+ GMV) are strong fits for Anthropic's growth environment, and the AI domain alignment is excellent. However, this role is fundamentally a Growth/Monetization PM — pricing strategy, subscription conversion funnels, packaging, and free-to-paid optimization — which is not where Riley's experience is concentrated. Riley's growth work at Kolon (2016–2021) is the closest match but is dated and consumer e-commerce, not SaaS/subscription monetization. There is no clear evidence of owning pricing, packaging, or paid conversion in a subscription business, which is the core requirement.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain and scale credentials, but the role demands deep subscription monetization and pricing ownership that Riley hasn't directly demonstrated.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth through data-driven experimentation, conversion optimization, and AI-powered product experiences at scale across Samsung.com, Amazon, and Kolon. Proven track record of defining KPIs, leading A/B testing programs, and building ML-driven systems that directly improve revenue metrics — including a 40% conversion lift in 12 weeks and a 25% AOV increase on a $2B+ GMV platform.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing
Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%, boosting 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
gap_handling: Riley lacks explicit SaaS/subscription monetization experience (pricing tiers, paid upgrade flows, consumption billing). To bridge this gap: (1) reframe the Kolon onboarding/retention bullet to emphasize its "free-to-first-purchase funnel" and monetization angle; (2) highlight the AEO pipeline's direct conversion impact (+40%) as a revenue-driving initiative; (3) in the cover letter, directly address the subscription gap by drawing a parallel between e-commerce conversion funnel ownership and SaaS free-to-paid mechanics, and express genuine passion for Anthropic's unique consumer + developer monetization challenge.

title: Product Manager, Consumer

The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.

top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

The 0-to-1 entrepreneurial build, governance layer design, and BI-competitive landscape research are also outside Riley's demonstrated scope.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, search ranking, recommendation systems — that directly map to Okta's "AI-native experiences" mandate. However, this is a Principal PM role requiring 10+ years, and Riley has 9 years, creating a slight seniority mismatch; more critically, the domain is enterprise identity/IAM and internal productivity tooling (IT, HR, Finance, GTM workflows), which is meaningfully different from Riley's consumer e-commerce and search expertise. The "agentic systems," "enterprise compliance," and "responsible AI" nice-to-haves are also largely absent from Riley's background, and the role is clearly a stretch above Riley's target seniority band (Senior PM / PM III).
recommendation_reason: Solid AI/ML product evidence makes Riley competitive, but the enterprise identity domain and Principal-level seniority bar are notable gaps requiring deliberate tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years of experience shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and intelligent automation at scale — from hybrid search systems serving 10M+ users to production ML models driving measurable enterprise outcomes. Proven track record taking AI products from 0→1 to broad adoption, defining rigorous evaluation frameworks, and partnering cross-functionally to translate complex AI capabilities into simple, high-impact user experiences.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users.

Defined an automated AEO strategy and LLM-driven pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%.
gap_handling: Okta's domain is enterprise identity (IAM), with the role focused on AI for internal workflows (IT, HR, Finance, GTM) — not consumer e-commerce. In the resume and cover letter, lean hard into the platform/infrastructure angle: the AEO pipeline, recommendation API, and conversational AI work are your best proxies for "AI-native enterprise product" experience. Acknowledge the enterprise context gap indirectly by emphasizing cross-functional stakeholder leadership, 0→1 delivery, and scale. For the seniority gap, position your Samsung PM III title and scope (10M+ users, international expansion, executive alignment) as Principal-equivalent impact even if the title doesn't match exactly.

title: Product Manager, Claude Code
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product depth and track record of shipping intelligent systems at scale (hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI) are genuinely relevant to Anthropic's AI-forward culture and demonstrate strong model-adjacent PM experience. However, the role is narrowly focused on developer tooling (CLI, agentic coding workflows) and explicitly requires at least 1 year of professional engineering experience — a hard requirement Riley's resume does not surface. Riley's background is squarely in consumer/e-commerce AI, not dev tools or technical developer-facing products, which is a meaningful domain gap for a role where "genuine love for developers" and hands-on coding tool fluency are core differentiators.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product credibility and Anthropic sponsorship are green flags, but the dev tools domain and required engineering background are real gaps that make this a competitive stretch.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to agentic pipelines and structured data extraction — for platforms serving 10M+ users. Deeply hands-on with model capabilities and AI toolchains, with a track record of translating cutting-edge research into high-impact, measurable product experiences in partnership with ML engineers and data scientists.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The role explicitly requires 1+ year of professional engineering experience — if Riley has any coding background (side projects, early-career technical work, coursework, or data/SQL-heavy work beyond analytics), it should be surfaced prominently in a cover letter or resume addendum. Riley should also demonstrate hands-on familiarity with Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or similar agentic dev tools — ideally as a daily user — and reference this concretely in the application to offset the dev tools domain gap.

title: Principal Product Manager, AI agents - Search
score_rationale: Riley's domain alignment is strong — AI-powered search, LLM pipelines, RAG-adjacent work (AEO, hybrid search, semantic ranking), and enterprise-scale product delivery all map well to Elastic's Agent Builder charter. However, this role explicitly requires 10+ years of PM experience for a Principal-level IC role, which is one level above Riley's target seniority (Senior/Staff PM), and Elastic's job description is geared toward a platform/developer-tools archetype (building agent infrastructure for technical users) rather than Riley's core strength in consumer-facing e-commerce discovery. Riley also lacks direct signals around RAG architectures, vector databases, context engineering, or hyperscaler partnerships — the core technical vocabulary Elastic is hiring for.
recommendation_reason: Strong domain overlap in AI search and LLMs, but the Principal-level seniority requirement and platform/developer-tools archetype are a real stretch from Riley's consumer/e-commerce discovery background.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience building and shipping AI-powered search, retrieval, and discovery systems at scale — including hybrid semantic search, LLM-driven pipelines, and conversational AI experiences serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven ability to partner with ML engineers and data scientists to define evaluation frameworks, relevance metrics, and agentic data extraction pipelines that deliver measurable business impact.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize relevance via A/B testing — resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined and delivered an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The most significant gap is the platform/developer-tools archetype: Elastic wants someone who has built infrastructure for technical users (developers building AI agents), not someone who has built consumer-facing search UX. Riley should explicitly connect AEO pipeline work and the conversational AI chat experience to "context engineering" and "agentic workflows" in the resume and cover letter. Also, Riley should name-drop RAG, vector databases, and retrieval architecture concepts (if genuinely familiar) to close the vocabulary gap — but only if honest. The 10-year requirement vs. Riley's ~9 years is a soft barrier, not a hard one.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths — AI/ML product delivery, data-driven experimentation, and cross-functional execution — are genuine assets here, and the 5+ year requirement and seniority level are a solid match. However, the role is squarely focused on B2B enterprise work management UX (project/task hierarchy, complex data models, collaboration workflows), which is a significant domain departure from Riley's AI search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization background. The preferred profile explicitly calls out "complex data models and visualizations, human-centric UX, and B2B platform/enterprise experience" — Riley has B2B exposure at Amazon but primarily in seller tooling, not collaborative productivity software. The NYC in-office requirement is also a location mismatch for a Bellevue-based candidate.
recommendation_reason: Domain mismatch (work management / enterprise productivity vs. AI search / e-commerce) and NYC hybrid office requirement are real friction points, but strong B2B/platform experience at Amazon and AI curiosity signals keep it a "maybe with tailoring."
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years delivering AI-powered platform experiences at Samsung and Amazon, with a consistent track record of translating complex technical systems — from ML ranking pipelines to multi-modal classification — into intuitive, high-impact products at enterprise scale. Adept at partnering with engineering, design, and data science cross-functionally to define bold roadmaps, drive rapid iteration, and ship with measurable business outcomes for millions of users.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ high-impact categories (Amazon)

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI; led phased rollout with A/B testing, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for B2B sellers, identifying key issues through 10+ interviews and 300+ surveys and coordinating solutions across 5 dependent teams (Amazon)
gap_handling: The biggest gap is domain fluency in collaborative work management / enterprise productivity software (task hierarchies, project views, compliance workflows). In your application and interviews, bridge this by emphasizing your Amazon B2B platform work — specifically the complexity of multi-party data models (seller inputs → catalog templates), self-serve workflow design, and stakeholder alignment across 5+ teams — as analogous to the systems-thinking demands of this role. Avoid leaning too hard on search/e-commerce; reframe your AI experience as "leveraging AI to simplify complex workflows for enterprise users."

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows, and multi-agent signals — that are directionally relevant. However, this role is squarely in enterprise identity security (IAM, OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust), a domain Riley has no documented experience in, which is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. Additionally, the role is developer-platform / B2B enterprise, a departure from Riley's consumer/e-commerce core. The LLM and AI agent awareness partially bridges the gap, but the identity infrastructure expertise is a meaningful missing piece.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product background is genuinely relevant, but the IAM/OAuth/OIDC domain expertise is a hard requirement Riley can't currently satisfy — needs honest gap acknowledgment.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and multi-agent workflows at Samsung and Amazon, reaching 10M+ monthly users. Passionate about the next frontier of AI agent identity and governance, bringing a deep track record of cross-functional technical collaboration, data-driven experimentation, and zero-to-one product delivery.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting AI-driven product discovery for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system using multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is IAM domain knowledge (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust). Before applying, Riley should invest a few hours in self-study — read Okta's developer docs on OAuth2/OIDC flows, skim the emerging MCP (Model Context Protocol) spec, and reference any API design or token-based auth work (even indirectly) in the resume. In the cover letter, frame the AEO pipeline and multi-agent workflow familiarity as a direct bridge to AI agent governance, and be upfront that the identity security domain is a learning edge — Okta may value the fresh AI product instincts over a traditional IAM background for this zero-to-one charter.

title: Product Manager, Developer Productivity
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: This role is a Developer Productivity / Platform Infrastructure PM at its core — requiring deep hands-on experience with build systems, CI/CD pipelines, monorepos, accelerator toolchains (GPU/TPU), and internal developer tooling. Riley's background is firmly rooted in consumer-facing AI search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization — an entirely different PM archetype. While Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials and understands AI-native development conceptually, there is no evidence of platform infrastructure, DevEx, or developer tooling ownership anywhere in the resume. The AI angle is genuinely compelling and earns some credit, but the domain and archetype mismatch is significant and structural.
recommendation_reason: Riley's consumer AI search and e-commerce discovery background has minimal overlap with the CI/CD, build systems, and developer platform infrastructure this role demands.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered systems — from LLM-driven pipelines and conversational AI to ML-based ranking and structured data extraction — that ship at scale and measurably improve how engineers and users interact with intelligent products. Deep experience defining metrics, running experiments, and driving AI product strategy across cross-functional teams at Samsung and Amazon, with a strong thesis on how agentic AI is reshaping how software is built and shipped.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The core gap is architectural: this role needs a PM who has lived inside a developer platform org — someone who has personally wrestled with Bazel build graphs, flaky CI pipelines, monorepo dependency hell, and GPU toolchain compatibility. Riley's ML pipeline work at Samsung (AEO pipeline, recommendation API) and Amazon (multi-modal classification pipelines) shows technical fluency, but none of it is developer-facing infrastructure. To credibly close this gap, Riley would need a bridge role in a developer tools or platform team first — or could consider adjacent Anthropic roles in AI product (Claude.ai, API/developer experience) that map far better to the actual background.

title: Senior Product Manager, APM - Observability
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths are in AI-powered search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization — domains that are meaningfully different from the APM/Observability/distributed tracing focus this role demands. While Elastic is a "Search AI Company" and Riley has strong search credentials, this role is squarely in DevOps/developer tooling (APM, Synthetics, RUM, OpenTelemetry) — a vertical with no evidence in Riley's resume. The seniority level and remote-US setup are good fits, but the domain gap is the dominant factor pulling the score down.
recommendation_reason: Hard domain mismatch — APM, distributed tracing, and observability tooling are not represented anywhere in Riley's background, making this a stretch that would require significant re-skilling narrative.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered data pipelines, structured metadata systems, and instrumentation strategies at scale — including behavioral signal instrumentation and search telemetry at Samsung.com for 10M+ monthly users. Bringing a customer-backwards product methodology and cross-functional execution track record to complex, data-rich developer-facing product domains.
top_bullets: Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, leveraging behavioral insights to iterate search experiences — increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system with multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: If Riley wants to pursue this role, the cover letter must directly acknowledge the domain shift and frame transferable signals: instrumentation strategy, telemetry pipeline ownership, and structured data work at Samsung/Amazon are the closest bridges. Riley would also benefit from demonstrating any hands-on familiarity with OpenTelemetry, Elastic Stack, or developer tooling in a side project or self-study — without that, the application will likely be screened out early by recruiters filtering for APM experience.

title: Senior Group Product Manager, Advertiser Optimization (Ads Marketplace)
score_rationale: This role is a Group Product Manager (people manager of PMs) focused squarely on Ads Marketplace — bidding, targeting, budget pacing, and advertiser-facing ad tech — a domain Riley has no direct experience in. Riley's strengths are deeply rooted in on-site search, discovery, and e-commerce personalization, which does not map to paid advertising infrastructure. Most critically, the role requires people management experience (hiring and developing a team of PMs), which is not evidenced anywhere in Riley's resume — a hard structural gap.
recommendation_reason: Role requires both Ads/ad-tech domain expertise and PM people management — two hard gaps not evidenced in Riley's background.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth through ML-powered personalization, search ranking, and recommendation systems at Samsung and Amazon. Experienced in cross-functional strategy, experimentation at scale, and translating complex data signals into high-impact product roadmaps for platforms serving millions of users.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%
Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: If Riley wants to pivot toward ads, the strongest bridge is the AEO pipeline and recommendation system work — both touch performance optimization and ML-driven automation. However, without any direct ads/bidding/auction experience or people management credentials, this specific GPM role is a two-level leap in the wrong direction. A better path would be targeting IC Senior PM roles at ad-tech adjacent companies (e.g., retail media, sponsored search at Amazon/Walmart) to build credibility before pursuing management-track ad tech roles.
